item.title: This is real footage from 120 years ago. None of the people in it knew that the city around them had four days left...
item.description: What you are watching is a cable car gliding down Market Street in San Francisco, filmed on the 14th of April, 1906. The camera was mounted on the front of the car, so you see the city exactly as it was: the crowds, the horse-drawn carriages, the early automobiles weaving through traffic, the men in hats, the great buildings rising on either side. An ordinary spring afternoon in a thriving American city. Four days later, on the morning of the 18th of April, a magnitude 7.9 earthquake struck. The shaking lasted under a minute, but it ignited fires that burned through the city for days... By the time it was over, more than 3,000 people were dead and roughly 80 percent of San Francisco had been destroyed. Almost every building you see in this footage was gone. And the film itself nearly went with it. The negative was placed on a train bound for New York on the 17th of April, the day before the earthquake. Had it left a single day later, it would have burned in the fire along with the studio that made it. This entire moving record of a lost city survives because of one day... ( seen on reddit r/interesting. Poster is bob-the-slob )
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: RIP to Sir Sam Neill ..... Brilliant actor, exemplary human being, absolute shock... Hunt for Red October, Peaky Blinders, Jurassic Park, Hunt for the Wilderpeople, and many many more.
item.description: He was currently embroiled in trying to stop mining in conservation areas in New Zealand. A great actor, great man.... https://www.1news.co.nz/2026/07/13/kiwi-screen-legend-sir-sam-neill-dies-aged-78/

item.title: Meme dump and call for help
item.description:
The saga continues
So E and I moved to a 1 bed hotel room following the previous post. If you need context, feel free to check my last meme dump
We are here with our 4 cats
Last week, I was only able to work half of my scheduled hours
No thanks to F. We needed to pack and move almost immediately
I was able to extend the stay until today
I only worked half my hours and thus, got half a paycheck
I can afford to extend it for another 4 days but I won't have enough for the week
E works at my job with me now and will be getting paid next week
I just won't be able to afford to exted it til then by myself
I'm sorry Imgur, I know its stupid to ask random strangers for help
But I'm desperate
If 6 of you send $50, I will be able to keep this room for another week
Hell, if 30 of you send $10, I would be in the clear
Especially since E gets paid next week
This week, I worked ~50 hours so I can be able to stay at the hotel with E and the cats
All my friends closest to me have already given all they can for this last week and I already feel bad for asking them in the first place
I hate being in such a desperate situation
And there's light at the end of the tunnel
But the tunnel is collapsing in on itself
If there is anything you guys can give at all
Please help E and myself out along with the cats
I appreciate you guys so much
Tl:dr - My roommate E and I have a hotel with our 4 cats. I only worked half of my scheduled hours since we were dealing with getting kicked out (see previous post). I've worked around 50 hours this week to compensate but I dont have enough to extend the hotel stay until we get paid next week. Asking kind souls on the internet for help, whatever you can give. Thank you for coming to my TED Talk
item.dc:creator: PharoahXero

Now do wake up dead man and HSM3
Pebbles is the size of a hamster
I just made her in tomodachi life
Bruce tax
Gertie tax
Nessie tax

item.title: GovBrief Today #528
item.description: The soldiers who survived one of the deadliest attacks on Americans in the Iran war just told the country that six of their own didn't have to die. Seventeen soldiers talked to The Washington Post about the drone strike at Port Shuaiba in Kuwait. They say their generals were briefed that the port was on an Iranian hit list, and the unit's own security assessments recommended against putting anyone there. The soldiers went where they were ordered, and a day into the war a drone hit them. The Army's investigation found no fault. The general who approved it is now the number two officer for the entire region. The wounded who landed in Germany were turned away from the largest American military hospital overseas because the Army did not list them as casualties. I've emailed 8,195 stories over 528 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260712-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july12 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/kuwait-attack [3] https://go.govbrief.today/graham-death [4] https://go.govbrief.today/mcconnell-statement [5] https://go.govbrief.today/ohio-responders [6] https://go.govbrief.today/houston-protest Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1747 (Romcom jobs)
item.description:
Let's get this bread
Forgot yesterday was Sunday and I didn't post a usual theme post. May do that as a bonus post today, or skip it for this week. Haven't decided. I guess we'll find out later!
Navajo Code talker names for countries.
Soras have that stereotype for some reason.
Pippin tax Today's message from Pippin: "Pippin! Pippin? Peekaboo!" ____ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Liz Oyer (@lawyer_oyer): We need to talk about antitrust law. It’s a crucial protection for consumers against corporate profiteering. DOJ is responsible for enforcement, but Todd Blanche’s Justice Department is refusing to do so.
item.description: Hurray for higher prices, lower quality and freedom of choice!

item.title: Trump Responsible for Cyclospora
item.description: This is 100% true In July 2025, the CDC scaled back its premier active tracking system for foodborne illnesses, FoodNet (the Foodborne Diseases Active Surveillance Network). The agency reduced the list of pathogens requiring strict, active tracking from eight down to just two.
item.dc:creator: Myballzzich

item.dc:creator: xcrit

item.title: "To practice any art, no matter how well or how badly, is a way to make your soul grow." — Kurt Vonnegut
item.description:
An art exhibition by Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez, whose work comments on issues facing U.S. Latinos, seemed like a natural fit for the campus of the University of North Texas in Denton, north of Dallas. Nearly a quarter of the students are Hispanic and the show focused on Quiñonez's dual cultural identity, as someone born in Ciudad Juárez, Mexico, and raised in East Dallas, Texas. Before it traveled to Texas, the show, called called "Ni De Aquí, Ni De Allá — Neither from Here Nor from There," had a successful run in Massachusetts at Boston University. But within a couple of days of its opening at UNT on Feb. 3, Quiñonez was being pinged on social media by students: The exhibition had been shut down. https://www.npr.org/2026/07/12/nx-s1-5783832/ice-pop-texas-victor-marka27-quinonez-artist
And per capita US gives the lowest amount to public arts https://intranet.americansforthearts.org/sites/default/files/Comparisonsofartsfunding27Oct2005_0.pdf
item.dc:creator: azazyel
